% Front Matter

Information about the current status of this document, any updates, and how to provide feedback may be found at {\DocURL}.}

\newcommand{\OTTNotice}{This document is subject to the Intellectual Property Rights (IPR) and Copyright policy of OSTERA (hereafter called "\textbf{Association}"). In addition, Members of the Association grant other Members of the Association certain intellectual property rights as set forth in the Association's IPR Policy.

Elements of this document may be subject to third party intellectual property rights, including without limitation, patent, copyright, trademark, or service mark rights, and any such third party may or may not be a Member of the Association. The Association takes no position regarding the validity or scope of any intellectual property or other rights that might be claimed to pertain to the implementation or use of the technology described in this document or the extent to which any license under such rights might or might not be available; neither does it represent that it has made any effort to identify any such rights. The Association is not responsible for, and shall not be held responsible in any manner for, identifying or failing to identify any or all such third-party intellectual property rights. The Association makes no representation that any information or list of intellectual property rights will at any time be complete, or that any patents in such a list are, in fact, standard essential patents. 

The Association requests that any party that believes it has patent claims or is aware of any claims of patent ownership of any kind that would necessarily be infringed by implementations of this deliverable to notify the Secretariat and/or President of the Association and to provide an indication of its willingness to grant patent licenses to such patent claims in a manner consistent with the IPR Policies of the Association. The Association may include such claims on its website but disclaims any obligation to do so.

The name "OSTERA" is a trademark of the Association, the owner and developer of this document, and should only be used to refer to the Association and its official outputs. Association trademarks, registered or otherwise, are the property of the Association. Their use shall be governed by such policies, procedures, and guidelines as shall be approved by the Association from time to time, and applicable law. All other company, brand, and product names in this document may be trademarks that are the sole property of their respective owners. The use of trademarks not owned by the Association shall be in accordance with applicable law and such contractual requirements as may be imposed by the owners of such trademarks.

This document and the information contained herein are provided on an “AS IS” basis and THE ASSOCIATION DISCLAIMS ALL WARRANTIES EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO (A) ANY WARRANTY THAT THE USE OF THE INFORMATION HEREIN WILL NOT INFRINGE ANY RIGHTS OF THIRD PARTIES (INCLUDING WITHOUT LIMITATION ANY INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS INCLUDING PATENT, COPYRIGHT, TRADEMARK, OR SERVICE MARK RIGHTS); OR (B) ANY IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, TITLE OR NONINFRINGEMENT. IN NO EVENT WILL THE ASSOCIATION BE LIABLE FOR ANY LOSS OF PROFITS, LOSS OF BUSINESS, LOSS OF USE OF DATA, INTERRUPTION OF BUSINESS, OR FOR ANY OTHER DIRECT, INDIRECT, SPECIAL OR EXEMPLARY, INCIDENTAL, PUNITIVE OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES OF ANY KIND, IN CONTRACT OR IN TORT, IN CONNECTION WITH THIS DOCUMENT OR THE INFORMATION CONTAINED HEREIN, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH LOSS OR DAMAGE.

% Section 3
\newcommand{\OTTAbbreviationsAndAcronyms}{This document uses the following abbreviations and acronyms.}

\newcommand{\OTTTermsDefinedElsewhere}{The following terms are used in this document, but are defined elsewhere. The summary definition for each term is provided purely as a convenience to the reader. If there is a difference between the summary and what is found in the document where it is defined, then the version where it is defined shall take precedence.}

\newcommand{\OTTTermsDefinedInThisDocument}{This document defines the following terms.}

% Section 4
\newcommand{\OTTNormativeTerms}{The following key words "\textbf{MUST}", "\textbf{MUST NOT}", "\textbf{REQUIRED}", "\textbf{SHALL}", "\textbf{SHALL NOT}", "\textbf{SHOULD}", "\textbf{SHOULD NOT}", "\textbf{RECOMMENDED}", "\textbf{NOT RECOMMENDED}", "\textbf{MAY}", and "\textbf{OPTIONAL}" in this document are to be interpreted as described below when, and only when, they appear in all capitals, as shown here.

\textbf{SHALL} - This word, or the terms "\textbf{MUST}" or "\textbf{REQUIRED}", means that the specific provisions are an absolute requirement of the specification and that they are to be followed strictly (no deviation is permitted).

\textbf{SHALL NOT} - This phrase, or the phrase "\textbf{MUST NOT}", means that the specific provision is absolutely prohibited.

\textbf{SHOULD} - This word, or the adjective "\textbf{RECOMMENDED}", means that a certain provision or course of action is preferred but not necessarily required as there may exist valid reasons in particular circumstances to ignore a particular item, but the full implications need to be understood and carefully weighed before choosing a different course. 

\textbf{SHOULD NOT} - This phrase, or the phrase "\textbf{NOT RECOMMENDED}" means that certain provision or course of action is undesirable but not prohibited as there may exist valid reasons in particular circumstances when the particular behavior is acceptable or even useful, but the full implications should be understood and the case carefully weighed before implementing any behavior described with this label.

\textbf{MAY} - This word, or the adjective "\textbf{OPTIONAL}", means that the provision is truly optional. One vendor may choose to include the item because a particular marketplace requires it or because the vendor feels that it enhances the product while another vendor may omit the same item. An implementation which does not include a particular option needs to be prepared to interoperate with another implementation which does include the option, though perhaps with reduced functionality. In the same vein an implementation which does include a particular option needs to be prepared to interoperate with another implementation which does not include the option (except, of course, for the feature the option provides.)
}
